title: शिव चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
जय गणेश गिरिजा सुवन, मंगल मूल सुजान।
कहत अयोध्यादास तुम, देहु अभय वरदान॥

जय गिरिजापति दीनदयाला।
सदा करत सन्तन प्रतिपाला॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय जय अनन्त अविनाशी।
करत कृपा सब के घट वासी॥

दुष्ट सकल नित मोहि सतावैं।
भ्रमत रहौं मोहि चैन न आवैं॥

त्राहि त्राहि मैं नाथ पुकारौं।
यहि अवसर मोहि आन उबारौं॥

लै त्रिशूल शत्रुन को मारो।
संकट से मोहि आन उबारो॥

माता पिता भ्राता सब कोई।
संकट में पूछत नहिं कोई॥

स्वामी एक है आस तुम्हारी।
आय हरहु अब संकट भारी॥

धन निर्धन को देत सदाहीं।
जो कोई जांचे वो फल पाहीं॥

अस्त-कोट रूप निरखत तेरा।
एक वृष आन अचम्भो मेरा॥

दोषन को दंडी नहिं कोई।
शम्भु विचारे बनत न सोई॥

धन्य-धन्य वह देह तुम्हारी।
सुन मन हरत विषम भवभारी॥

एक समय तुम पार्वति संगा।
बैठे थे करते अनुरागा॥

विष्णुजी बैठे सोचत ठाढ़े।
मुनिजन पूजत सिद्ध अगाड़ी॥

त्रिगुण रूप निरखत जग सारा।
तुम समान कोई नाहिं संसारा॥

महिमा कहाँ तुम्हारी जाई।
गंगाधर बात कहत न बनाई॥

शिव पार्वती की जय बोलो।
दोनों कृपा दृष्टि से तोलो॥

कृपा दृष्टि जब करो हमारी।
संकट कटत मिटत भवभारी॥

त्रिभुवन मातु चन्द्रमुख चारी।
तुम्हें बिना सुख पावे नाहीं कोई नर-नारी॥

केवल पार्वती संग तिहारे।
यह आनंद मंगल सुख सारे॥

शम्भु सदा तुम पर बलिहारी।
करत हैं शम्भू कष्ट निवारी॥

नंदी भृंगी बिन पग धारी।
कंचन मेरु कनक छबि न्यारी॥

कनकसभा करते रावण पूजा।
भोलेनाथ और नहिं दूजा॥

कामधेनु गृह करत उजारा।
तीनों लोक में सबसे न्यारा॥

जो ध्यावे फल पावे सोई।
दुख न ताके निकट रहे कोई॥

वृषभ चढ़े शिव चले पठारी।
करत त्रिशूल ताड़ संहारी॥

गलसुन्दर मृगछाल विराजे।
विषधर भुजंग कंठ में साजे॥

जा पर कृपा करे रघुराई।
ता पर कृपा करे शम्भु मेरे भाई॥

शंकर हो संकट के नाशा।
मंगल मूरत रूप विलासा॥

विघ्न विनाशन मंगल कारी।
सुख सम्पत्ति करो भवतारी॥

पार्वती अम्बे मैया जगदम्बा।
भोले शम्भु सदा सुखदम्बा॥

देवन के देवा महादेवा।
तन धर माथे लगत फूल बेला॥

मुनि महेश की करौ भक्ती।
सदा करौ शम्भु की रक्ती॥

शम्भु कर चढ़ो विशेष पूजा।
तब ही कटे सब दुख का दूजा॥

बूंद-बूंद चढ़ाओ जलधारा।
सुख सम्पत्ति हो सब संसारा॥

शम्भू को चढ़ाओ बिल्वपत्र।
तुरंत मिले शिव दर्शन सर्वत्र॥

चन्दन चढ़ाओ त्रिपुरारी।
शिवजी की कृपा हो अपारी॥

भाँग-धतूर चढ़ाओ भोले।
सर्व सिद्धि करो शिव तोले॥

ग्रह कलेश दुख मिटावो।
सुख सम्पत्ति भक्ति घर लावो॥

जहाँ कहीं पीर शम्भु धरावो।
वहाँ-वहाँ अपना भक्त बचावो॥

॥ दोहा ॥
नित्त नेम करि प्रातःकाल, पाठ करौ चालीसा।
तुम मेरी मनोकामना, पूर्ण करो जगदीशा॥

मगसर छठि हेमन्त ऋतु, सम्वत् चौसठ जान।
स्तुति चालीसा शिव करी, पूर्ण कीन कल्याण॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Jaya Ganesha Girija Suvana, Mangala Mula Sujana.
Kahata Ayodhyadasa Tuma, Dehu Abhaya Varadana.

Jaya Girijapati Dinadayala.
Sada Karata Santana Pratipala.

|| Chaupai ||
Jaya Jaya Jaya Ananta Avinashi.
Karata Kripa Saba Ke Ghata Vasi.

Dushta Sakala Nita Mohi Satavain.
Bhramata Rahon Mohi Chaina Na Avain.

Trahi Trahi Main Natha Pukaraun.
Yahi Avasara Mohi Ana Ubaraun.

Lai Trishula Shatrun Ko Maro.
Sankata Se Mohi Ana Ubaro.

Mata Pita Bhrata Saba Koi.
Sankata Mein Puchata Nahin Koi.

Swami Eka Hai Asa Tumhari.
Aya Harahu Aba Sankata Bhari.

Dhana Nirdhana Ko Deta Sadahin.
Jo Koi Janche Vo Phala Pahin.

Asta-Kota Rupa Nirakhata Tera.
Eka Vrisha Ana Achambo Mera.

Doshana Ko Dandi Nahin Koi.
Shambhu Vichare Banata Na Soi.

Dhanya Dhanya Vaha Deha Tumhari.
Suna Mana Harata Vishama Bhavabhari.

Eka Samaya Tuma Parvati Sanga.
Baithe The Karate Anuraga.

Vishnuji Baithe Sochata Thadhe.
Munijana Pujata Siddha Agadhi.

Triguna Rupa Nirakhata Jaga Sara.
Tuma Samana Koi Nahin Sansara.

Mahima Kahan Tumhari Jai.
Gangadhara Bata Kahata Na Banai.

Shiva Parvati Ki Jaya Bolo.
Donon Kripa Drishti Se Tolo.

Kripa Drishti Jaba Karo Hamari.
Sankata Katata Mitata Bhavabhari.

Tribhuvana Matu Chandramukha Chari.
Tumhen Bina Sukha Pave Nahin Koi Nara-Nari.

Kevala Parvati Sanga Tihare.
Yaha Ananda Mangala Sukha Sare.

Shambhu Sada Tuma Para Balihari.
Karata Hain Shambhu Kashta Nivari.

Nandi Bhringi Bina Paga Dhari.
Kanchana Meru Kanaka Chhabi Nyari.

Kanakasabha Karate Ravana Puja.
Bholenatha Aura Nahin Duja.

Kamadhenu Griha Karata Ujara.
Tinon Loka Mein Sabase Nyara.

Jo Dhyave Phala Pave Soi.
Dukha Na Take Nikata Rahe Koi.

Vrishabha Chadhe Shiva Chale Pathari.
Karata Trishula Tada Sanhari.

Galasundara Mrigachhala Viraje.
Vishadhara Bhujanga Kantha Mein Saje.

Ja Para Kripa Kare Raghurai.
Ta Para Kripa Kare Shambhu Mere Bhai.

Shankara Ho Sankata Ke Nasha.
Mangala Murata Rupa Vilasa.

Vighna Vinashana Mangala Kari.
Sukha Sampatti Karo Bhavatari.

Parvati Ambe Maiya Jagadamba.
Bhole Shambhu Sada Sukhadamba.

Devana Ke Deva Mahadeva.
Tana Dhara Mathe Lagata Phula Bela.

Muni Mahesha Ki Karau Bhakti.
Sada Karau Shambhu Ki Rakti.

Shambhu Kara Chadho Vishesha Puja.
Taba Hi Kate Saba Dukha Ka Duja.

Bunda Bunda Chadhao Jaladhara.
Sukha Sampatti Ho Saba Sansara.

Shambhu Ko Chadhao Bilvapatra.
Turanta Mile Shiva Darshana Sarvatra.

Chandana Chadhao Tripurari.
Shivaji Ki Kripa Ho Apari.

Bhanga Dhatura Chadhao Bhole.
Sarva Siddhi Karo Shiva Tole.

Graha Kalesha Dukha Mitavo.
Sukha Sampatti Bhakti Ghara Lavo.

Jahan Kahin Pira Shambhu Dharavo.
Vahan Vahan Apana Bhakta Bachavo.

|| Doha ||
Nitta Nema Kari Pratahkala, Patha Karau Chalisa.
Tuma Meri Manokamana, Purna Karo Jagadisha.

Magsara Chhathi Hemanta Ritu, Samvat Chausatha Jana.
Stuti Chalisa Shiva Kari, Purna Kina Kalyana.
hindiMeaning: शिव चालीसा में भगवान शिव की महिमा का वर्णन है। इसमें भोलेनाथ से संकट हरण, दुष्टों का नाश, धन-सम्पत्ति और सुख की प्रार्थना की गई है। भक्त कहता है कि माता-पिता और भाई सब हैं, पर संकट में कोई नहीं पूछता — केवल शिव ही एकमात्र आश्रय हैं। त्रिशूलधारी शिव शत्रुओं का नाश करें और भक्त को संकट से उबारें। गंगाधर शिव की महिमा अकथनीय है। पार्वती सहित शिव की कृपा से सभी विघ्न दूर होते हैं और सुख-सम्पत्ति प्राप्त होती है। बिल्वपत्र, चन्दन, जलधारा से शिव पूजन करने पर सर्व सिद्धि और ग्रह-क्लेश निवारण होता है। प्रातःकाल नियमपूर्वक इस चालीसा का पाठ करने से सभी मनोकामनाएँ पूर्ण होती हैं।
benefits: Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and wishes of the devotee
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles, sorrows, and fears
Invokes wealth, prosperity, and material comforts
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and evil forces
Traditionally associated with peace of mind and spiritual upliftment
Daily recitation brings Lord Shiva's divine blessings
Traditionally recited for removal of the effects of negative planetary influences
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Shiv Chalisa (शिव चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. Drawn from Traditional Hindi Devotional Composition, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bring peace of mind and spiritual upliftment as well as destroy the effects of negative planetary influences. Additionally, it is known to remove all obstacles, sorrows, and fears. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month. With 40 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shiv Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiv Chalisa in Hindi with meaning and English transliteration. Powerful 40-verse prayer to Lord Shiva for protection, wealth, and peace.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Shiv Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiv Chalisa is traditionally attributed to Pandit Ayodhya Das (also written as Ayodhyadas), a Hindi devotional poet whose name appears in the closing verses of the chalisa as a humble petitioner asking Shiva for fearlessness and removal of worries. Like other vernacular chalisas of the medieval and early modern period, the text was composed in Khari Boli Hindi to make Shiva-bhakti accessible to non-Sanskrit speaking devotees. The work draws thematic material from the larger Shiva Purana, attributed to Veda Vyasa, and condenses key episodes — Sati's self-immolation, Shiva's marriage to Parvati, the swallowing of halahala poison, the Tripura Samhara, and the blessings to devotees like Markandeya — into 40 chaupais. (Sources: Devshoppe; Mahashivratri.org)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Shiv Chalisa and how long does it take to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiv Chalisa follows the canonical chalisa form: an opening doha, forty chaupais, and a closing doha — making 43 verses in total, the same architecture as the Hanuman Chalisa. A single careful recitation takes approximately 8 to 10 minutes, depending on the pace. Many devotees combine it with the 108-name Shiva Ashtottara, the Mahamrityunjaya mantra, and the Lingashtakam to form a complete daily Shiva path of about 30 minutes. During Sawan Mondays and on Maha Shivratri, devotees often recite the chalisa 5, 11, 21, or 108 times. The repetitive chaupai meter is designed for easy memorisation and group chanting. (Source: Sanatana Journey; Shiv Chalisa English PDF)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Monday considered the most powerful day to recite Shiv Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Monday (Somvar) is dedicated to Lord Shiva across all Hindu traditions because the moon (Soma/Chandra) adorns Shiva's matted hair, and the planet Moon is associated with this day. Reciting Shiv Chalisa on Monday morning after a bath, with bilva (bel) leaves, white flowers, and water offered on the Shivalinga, is considered the most efficacious daily Shiva sadhana. The practice intensifies during the lunar month of Sawan (Shravan, July-August), when each Monday is called Sawan Somvar; many devotees observe a fast and recite the chalisa multiple times. Sixteen consecutive Mondays of recitation, called Solah Somvar Vrat, is undertaken by many young women for marriage and family welfare. (Source: JIPanditji Sawan Somvar; Sanatana Journey)

seoFaqs.question: What is the special significance of reciting Shiv Chalisa during Sawan and Maha Shivratri?
seoFaqs.answer: Sawan (the lunar month of Shravan, July-August) is considered the holiest month for Shiva worship because, according to the Samudra Manthan story, Shiva consumed the halahala poison during this period to save creation. Each Monday of Sawan — Sawan Somvar — is treated as a major fast and worship day, with devotees offering jal abhishek to the Shivalinga and reciting the chalisa 11 or 21 times. Maha Shivratri, the 'great night of Shiva' on the Krishna Paksha Chaturdashi of Phalgun (February-March), is the single most important Shiva festival; devotees stay awake through the four praharas of the night, perform abhishek with milk, honey, and curd, and recite the chalisa repeatedly to honour the night Shiva is said to have danced the Tandava. (Source: JIPanditji)

seoFaqs.question: What worldly and spiritual benefits does the Shiv Chalisa promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiv Chalisa explicitly promises that sincere recitation grants removal of fear, freedom from sins, peace of mind, prosperity, good health, success in legal and family matters, and ultimately moksha (liberation from the cycle of birth and death). Several chaupais cite specific examples — Markandeya being saved from Yama, Banasura receiving Shiva's grace, Ravana obtaining boons through tapasya — as historical proof that Shiva grants whatever a devotee sincerely asks. Modern devotees commonly recite the chalisa for marital harmony, recovery from illness, relief from Shani Sade Sati, and the well-being of children. The closing verses make clear that the highest fruit, beyond worldly gains, is undivided devotion to Shiva himself. (Sources: Astroyogi; Astromantra)
